entry.title: Creating BOM with new Usage instead of Usage 'S' within Table STZU
entry.content:
When creating a Task List via IA05, a Material is added to an Operation which creates a BOM in the background.
When you check Table STZU, the 'Usage' is maintained as '4' (Plant Maintenance).
How-To configure this to be another (new) Usage-Type  instead of '4' (Plant Maintenance)?

It has BOM-Type S, can it be linked with this Type?

entry.title: Object table redraw is not working
entry.content:
HI Experts,

Object table redraw is not removing the previously highlighted item selection. In the below video, I am resetting the data, if the user selects the left/right arrow from the action bar item. The data is set correctly after the redraw but it still holds the previously highlighted item. 

How to remove the selection ? is there any property I can use to change the selection ? 

@bill_froelich @Jitendra_Kansal any help to resolve this issue?

Thanks,

Ram

entry.title: Re: Build Application using Python - SAP UI5 & Deploy to SAP BTP
entry.content:
@krishnakumar_nagarajan01 @monapiao 

Here is the Repo

https://github.com/yogananda-muthaiah/Python-SAP-UI5/

entry.title: Re: Authoring SAP Companion Content - About Author Permis...
entry.content:
Hi Angad,

I guess the parameter "help-editor=true" is enabled in your S/4 Hana by Basis. And that's why all users are seeing pencil icon.

entry.title: Re: Loading data to Planning enabled Model through Analytical application Scripting
entry.content: Are you using the custom widget option mentioned in this blog? https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/how-to-upload-data-to-a-public-or-private-planning-version-using-a-file/ba-p/13576559

entry.title: Embedded SAC language is different from C4C
entry.content:
logged in German, C4C runs in German, if C4C is set to English, eSAC runs in English, but changed to German if switched in C4C, row an column headers are in German as eSAC UI s still in English. Is there a way to change language for eSAC UI?

Thank you very much,

Best Regards, Uwe

entry.title: Re: SAP MDG: Full Forms of column names in Mapping Sheet
entry.content: Tysm 🙂

entry.title: About using custom fonts in custom form templates
entry.content:
I would like to know how to set a custom font in a custom template using the "Embed PDF" property.
I have already uploaded the custom font to Adobe Document Services (ADS). It is also installed on my Windows machine.
Are there any restrictions on custom fonts?

I would also like to know how to set a custom form template in a specified report.

Thank you in advance.

SAP Forms service by Adobe 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Extensibility 

NW ABAP Print and Output Management

entry.title: Objects are imported but not generated when moved to production system
entry.content:
Hello,

The objects of Odata service and Odata project where locked in a TR and the TR was moved from Dev to Quality successfully. When the same TR was imported to Production system, all objects except odata service and few table entries were successfully imported but not generated or activated. 

How to generate or activate these objects now.

The screenshot have been attached herewith.

entry.title: Re: how many modules in SAP
entry.content:
Core Modules and Sub-Modules

Each core module has multiple sub-modules. Here is an expanded view:

  1. FI - Financial Accounting

    • FI-GL (General Ledger Accounting)
    • FI-AP (Accounts Payable)
    • FI-AR (Accounts Receivable)
    • FI-AA (Asset Accounting)
    • FI-BL (Bank Accounting)
    • FI-SL (Special Purpose Ledger)
    • FI-TV (Travel Management)

  2. CO - Controlling

    • CO-OM (Overhead Management)
    • CO-CCA (Cost Center Accounting)
    • CO-OPA (Internal Orders)
    • CO-ABC (Activity-Based Costing)
    • CO-PC (Product Cost Controlling)
    • CO-PA (Profitability Analysis)
    • CO-PCA (Profit Center Accounting)

  3. AM - Asset Management

    • AM-AS (Asset Accounting)
    • AM-AU (Asset Transfer)
    • AM-AV (Asset Valuation)
    • AM-IM (Investment Management)

  4. PS - Project Systems

    • PS-ST (Structures)
    • PS-PL (Planning)
    • PS-CN (Execution)
    • PS-RM (Resources)
    • PS-BS (Reporting and Analysis)

  5. HR - Human Resources

    • HR-PA (Personnel Administration)
    • HR-OM (Organizational Management)
    • HR-PT (Time Management)
    • HR-PY (Payroll)
    • HR-TM (Talent Management)
    • HR-EC (Employee Central in SuccessFactors)

  6. PM - Plant Maintenance

    • PM-EQM (Equipment and Technical Objects)
    • PM-PRM (Preventive Maintenance)
    • PM-WCM (Work Clearance Management)

  7. MM - Materials Management

    • MM-PUR (Purchasing)
    • MM-IM (Inventory Management)
    • MM-IV (Invoice Verification)
    • MM-WM (Warehouse Management)

  8. QM - Quality Management

    • QM-PT (Quality Planning)
    • QM-IN (Quality Inspection)
    • QM-QC (Quality Control)
    • QM-CA (Quality Certificates)

  9. PP - Production Planning

    • PP-SOP (Sales and Operations Planning)
    • PP-MRP (Material Requirements Planning)
    • PP-CRP (Capacity Requirements Planning)
    • PP-PI (Process Industries)

  10. SD - Sales and Distribution

    • SD-MD (Master Data)
    • SD-SLS (Sales)
    • SD-SHP (Shipping)
    • SD-BIL (Billing)
    • SD-FTT (Foreign Trade)

  11. LE - Logistics Execution

    • LE-WM (Warehouse Management)
    • LE-SHP (Shipping)
    • LE-TRA (Transportation)

  12. WM - Warehouse Management

    • WM-MD (Master Data)
    • WM-TM (Task and Resource Management)
    • WM-IM (Inventory Management)

  13. EWM - Extended Warehouse Management

    • EWM-BF (Basic Functions)
    • EWM-ILO (Inbound Logistics)
    • EWM-OLO (Outbound Logistics)

  14. TRM - Treasury and Risk Management

    • TRM-CM (Cash Management)
    • TRM-IM (In-House Cash)
    • TRM-RM (Risk Management)

  15. FM - Funds Management

    • FM-BU (Budgeting)
    • FM-CO (Control)
    • FM-RE (Reporting)

  16. IM - Investment Management

    • IM-PL (Planning)
    • IM-PR (Project Control)
    • IM-RE (Real Estate Management)

  17. LO - Logistics

    • LO-MD (Master Data)
    • LO-SP (Service Parts)
    • LO-DS (Distribution)

  18. WF - SAP Business Workflow

    • WF-DS (Design)
    • WF-EX (Execution)
    • WF-MN (Monitoring)

  19. CRM - Customer Relationship Management

    • CRM-MKT (Marketing)
    • CRM-SAL (Sales)
    • CRM-SRV (Service)

  20. SRM - Supplier Relationship Management

    • SRM-SUS (Supplier Self-Services)
    • SRM-EBP (Enterprise Buyer)
    • SRM-SLC (Supplier Lifecycle Management)

  21. SCM - Supply Chain Management

    • SCM-APO (Advanced Planning and Optimization)
    • SCM-EWM (Extended Warehouse Management)
    • SCM-EM (Event Management)

  22. PLM - Product Lifecycle Management

    • PLM-PR (Product Data Management)
    • PLM-CP (Change Management)
    • PLM-EC (Engineering Change)

  23. APO - Advanced Planner and Optimizer

    • APO-DP (Demand Planning)
    • APO-SNP (Supply Network Planning)
    • APO-PPDS (Production Planning/Detailed Scheduling)

  24. EHS - Environment, Health, and Safety

    • EHS-MP (Management of Hazardous Substances)
    • EHS-RC (Regulatory Compliance)

  25. SEM - Strategic Enterprise Management

    • SEM-CPM (Corporate Performance Management)
    • SEM-BCS (Business Consolidation)

  26. BI - Business Intelligence

    • BI-BW (Business Warehouse)
    • BI-IP (Integrated Planning)
    • BI-BO (BusinessObjects)

  27. BW - Business Information Warehouse

    • BW-ETL (Extraction, Transformation, Loading)
    • BW-OLAP (Online Analytical Processing)

  28. GRC - Governance, Risk, and Compliance

    • GRC-AC (Access Control)
    • GRC-RM (Risk Management)
    • GRC-PC (Process Control)

  29. MDM - Master Data Management

    • MDM-CE (Content Enrichment)
    • MDM-IM (Information Management)
    • MDM-GS (Global Data Synchronization)

  30. HCM - Human Capital Management

    • HCM-PA (Personnel Administration)
    • HCM-OM (Organizational Management)
    • HCM-TM (Time Management)
    • HCM-PY (Payroll)
    • HCM-EC (Employee Central in SuccessFactors)

entry.title: Re: What's the difference between "?=" and "CAST"?
entry.content:
CAST can be used in-line; typically during method calls. Example:

my_method( obj = CAST #( lo_something ) ).

entry.title: Re: All SAP Modules List (Total == 64 ?)
entry.content:
Core Modules and Sub-Modules

Each core module has multiple sub-modules. Here is an expanded view:

  1. FI - Financial Accounting

    • FI-GL (General Ledger Accounting)
    • FI-AP (Accounts Payable)
    • FI-AR (Accounts Receivable)
    • FI-AA (Asset Accounting)
    • FI-BL (Bank Accounting)
    • FI-SL (Special Purpose Ledger)
    • FI-TV (Travel Management)

  2. CO - Controlling

    • CO-OM (Overhead Management)
    • CO-CCA (Cost Center Accounting)
    • CO-OPA (Internal Orders)
    • CO-ABC (Activity-Based Costing)
    • CO-PC (Product Cost Controlling)
    • CO-PA (Profitability Analysis)
    • CO-PCA (Profit Center Accounting)

  3. AM - Asset Management

    • AM-AS (Asset Accounting)
    • AM-AU (Asset Transfer)
    • AM-AV (Asset Valuation)
    • AM-IM (Investment Management)

  4. PS - Project Systems

    • PS-ST (Structures)
    • PS-PL (Planning)
    • PS-CN (Execution)
    • PS-RM (Resources)
    • PS-BS (Reporting and Analysis)

  5. HR - Human Resources

    • HR-PA (Personnel Administration)
    • HR-OM (Organizational Management)
    • HR-PT (Time Management)
    • HR-PY (Payroll)
    • HR-TM (Talent Management)
    • HR-EC (Employee Central in SuccessFactors)

  6. PM - Plant Maintenance

    • PM-EQM (Equipment and Technical Objects)
    • PM-PRM (Preventive Maintenance)
    • PM-WCM (Work Clearance Management)

  7. MM - Materials Management

    • MM-PUR (Purchasing)
    • MM-IM (Inventory Management)
    • MM-IV (Invoice Verification)
    • MM-WM (Warehouse Management)

  8. QM - Quality Management

    • QM-PT (Quality Planning)
    • QM-IN (Quality Inspection)
    • QM-QC (Quality Control)
    • QM-CA (Quality Certificates)

  9. PP - Production Planning

    • PP-SOP (Sales and Operations Planning)
    • PP-MRP (Material Requirements Planning)
    • PP-CRP (Capacity Requirements Planning)
    • PP-PI (Process Industries)

  10. SD - Sales and Distribution

    • SD-MD (Master Data)
    • SD-SLS (Sales)
    • SD-SHP (Shipping)
    • SD-BIL (Billing)
    • SD-FTT (Foreign Trade)

  11. LE - Logistics Execution

    • LE-WM (Warehouse Management)
    • LE-SHP (Shipping)
    • LE-TRA (Transportation)

  12. WM - Warehouse Management

    • WM-MD (Master Data)
    • WM-TM (Task and Resource Management)
    • WM-IM (Inventory Management)

  13. EWM - Extended Warehouse Management

    • EWM-BF (Basic Functions)
    • EWM-ILO (Inbound Logistics)
    • EWM-OLO (Outbound Logistics)

  14. TRM - Treasury and Risk Management

    • TRM-CM (Cash Management)
    • TRM-IM (In-House Cash)
    • TRM-RM (Risk Management)

  15. FM - Funds Management

    • FM-BU (Budgeting)
    • FM-CO (Control)
    • FM-RE (Reporting)

  16. IM - Investment Management

    • IM-PL (Planning)
    • IM-PR (Project Control)
    • IM-RE (Real Estate Management)

  17. LO - Logistics

    • LO-MD (Master Data)
    • LO-SP (Service Parts)
    • LO-DS (Distribution)

  18. WF - SAP Business Workflow

    • WF-DS (Design)
    • WF-EX (Execution)
    • WF-MN (Monitoring)

  19. CRM - Customer Relationship Management

    • CRM-MKT (Marketing)
    • CRM-SAL (Sales)
    • CRM-SRV (Service)

  20. SRM - Supplier Relationship Management

    • SRM-SUS (Supplier Self-Services)
    • SRM-EBP (Enterprise Buyer)
    • SRM-SLC (Supplier Lifecycle Management)

  21. SCM - Supply Chain Management

    • SCM-APO (Advanced Planning and Optimization)
    • SCM-EWM (Extended Warehouse Management)
    • SCM-EM (Event Management)

  22. PLM - Product Lifecycle Management

    • PLM-PR (Product Data Management)
    • PLM-CP (Change Management)
    • PLM-EC (Engineering Change)

  23. APO - Advanced Planner and Optimizer

    • APO-DP (Demand Planning)
    • APO-SNP (Supply Network Planning)
    • APO-PPDS (Production Planning/Detailed Scheduling)

  24. EHS - Environment, Health, and Safety

    • EHS-MP (Management of Hazardous Substances)
    • EHS-RC (Regulatory Compliance)

  25. SEM - Strategic Enterprise Management

    • SEM-CPM (Corporate Performance Management)
    • SEM-BCS (Business Consolidation)

  26. BI - Business Intelligence

    • BI-BW (Business Warehouse)
    • BI-IP (Integrated Planning)
    • BI-BO (BusinessObjects)

  27. BW - Business Information Warehouse

    • BW-ETL (Extraction, Transformation, Loading)
    • BW-OLAP (Online Analytical Processing)

  28. GRC - Governance, Risk, and Compliance

    • GRC-AC (Access Control)
    • GRC-RM (Risk Management)
    • GRC-PC (Process Control)

  29. MDM - Master Data Management

    • MDM-CE (Content Enrichment)
    • MDM-IM (Information Management)
    • MDM-GS (Global Data Synchronization)

  30. HCM - Human Capital Management

    • HCM-PA (Personnel Administration)
    • HCM-OM (Organizational Management)
    • HCM-TM (Time Management)
    • HCM-PY (Payroll)
    • HCM-EC (Employee Central in SuccessFactors)

See hopefully soon again!

entry.title: How to use GL Upload and BDC coding field in SAP VIM?
entry.content:
VIM G/L Upload

 

 

 

 

How to make sure BDC Data is transferred in line item based on AFS Id

 

Please check below Playlist for SAP VIM

 

https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL5LE1pk3CSPqbjN-C9qNCRxJp5jo3iQjh

entry.title: Re: FLP: Should we use CONF or CUST scope for Technical/B...
entry.content:
Hi Ramin

Custom technical catalogs always client-independent. That is original entries that are pointing to the actual program/application that runs the app/UI. Tool: Launchpad App Manager

Custom business catalogs are usually client-specific. That is references to technical catalogs entries, so that you can assign them to business roles that are always client-specific. Tool: Launchpad Content Manager.

Refer to the official best practices in the SAP Fiori launchpad guide 

https://help.sap.com/docs/ABAP_PLATFORM_NEW/a7b390faab1140c087b8926571e942b7/6ceb3b39e4af4241acde015fece66e26.html?version=202310.001

entry.title: Re: FLP: Should we use CONF or CUST scope for Technical/B...
entry.content: Hi Ramin, Best to just use client-specific for business catalogs in all cases. There's no advantage in using client-independent (CONF) scope here and if you have to add another client later for some reason you will lose flexibility. Also most of the really helpful features of Launchpad Content Manger are in the client-specific tool... which should be a clue in itself!

entry.title: Re: SAP Enterprise Messaging for SAP ERP: HowTo-Guide (Part 2 - First use case)
entry.content:
Hi, 

@martin_schffler    I have copied the standard Sales order business object BUS2023 into ZZBUS2032. Also created a custom message type ZSO_CREATE. After completing all the steps as mentioned (SWE2 also maintained), the /ASADEV/ACI_MONITOR does not show any events triggered for Sales order creation.

For sale order change the vents are getting triggered but the events are not reaching the event mesh.

 

SWE2 settings

ACI monitor showing events triggered for the Sales order changes.

 

Your help is highly appreciated!

 

Thanks,

Smohan

entry.title: Re: Error while extending Fiori App "Manage Maintenance Backlog". Error : Navigation Not a
entry.content: Hi Priyanka Unfortunately that's not enough information for anyone to help you resolve it. You need to include some details such as: Which SAP Product suite (SAP S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Cloud) and which release/FPS? What have you tried so far? What were you doing when you got the error? Most likely it's an authorizations issue so see if you have App Support option listed in the User Actions menu.

entry.title: Re: Migration tool connection to SAP PO
entry.content: Hi Sai, Thank you for the response! It was due to another issue and it got solved.

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere connection with SAP ECC and SAP CRM
entry.content: Thanks @TuncayKaraca. For delta extraction, do you suggest BW Bridge or SAP DI can be used?

entry.title: The installation of SAP products failed with 5 error(s)
entry.content:
Hi Expert

An error occurred during the installation of SAP.

 


Below is the content of the log file

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<?xml-stylesheet type="text/xsl" href="SapSetupError.xsl"?>
<SapSetup Context="ErrorLog">
<ErrorList>
<Error>
<Description>Registration failed.<p/></Description>
<ErrorCode>0x80004005</ErrorCode>
<Dest>C:\Program Files (x86)\SAP\FrontEnd\SapGui\GridView.ocx</Dest>
<Source>C:\Users\tanaka\AppData\Local\Temp\Sap8F24.tmp\SapGui\GridView.ocx</Source>
<State>Install</State>
<Type>File</Type>
</Error>
<Error>
<Description>Registration failed.<p/></Description>
<ErrorCode>0x80004005</ErrorCode>
<Dest>C:\Program Files (x86)\SAP\FrontEnd\SapGui\sapcalen.ocx</Dest>
<Source>C:\Users\tanaka\AppData\Local\Temp\Sap8F24.tmp\SapGui\sapcalen.ocx</Source>
<State>Install</State>
<Type>File</Type>
</Error>
<Error>
<Description>Component $CoreGUI Files is in error. It has not been successfully installed.<p/></Description>
<ErrorCode>0x80004005</ErrorCode>
<Dest/>
<Source>{4FA50AAA-25C2-4829-B605-D2F2E0FD0401}</Source>
<State>Install</State>
<Type>Component</Type>
</Error>
<Error>
<Description>Registration failed.<p/></Description>
<ErrorCode>0x80004005</ErrorCode>
<Dest>C:\Program Files (x86)\SAP\FrontEnd\SapGui\apogrid.ocx</Dest>
<Source>C:\Users\tanaka\AppData\Local\Temp\Sap8F24.tmp\SapGui\apogrid.ocx</Source>
<State>Install</State>
<Type>File</Type>
</Error>
<Error>
<Description>Component $SCM is in error. It has not been successfully installed.<p/></Description>
<ErrorCode>0x80004005</ErrorCode>
<Dest/>
<Source>{23B2DA68-7F1B-4ECC-BAE8-84C9B361C27F}</Source>
<State>Install</State>
<Type>Component</Type>
</Error>
</ErrorList>
<Time>10:34:36</Time>
<Date>25.04.2024</Date>
<UserName>tanaka</UserName>
<ComputerName>50500-TANAKA</ComputerName>
</SapSetup>

What is the problem?
Please tell us.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: Jobs running by FFID
entry.content:
Dear Sankar,

As per as I know, you can create a system user as "Joubuser" and you can use this user for background jobs. 

For 

https://help.sap.com/doc/saphelp_nw73ehp1/7.31.19/en-US/4b/2b2954365474fee10000000a421937/content.htm?no_cache=true

 

https://www.esds.co.in/kb/how-to-schedule-background-job-in-sap/#:~:text=Execute%20SM37%20T%2Dcode%20Select,(Released%20%3E%3E%20Scheduled).&text=Click%20on%20the%20edit%20option,definition%20and%20scheduling%20are%20completed.

Best Regards

entry.content:
Hi,

I am also facing same issue can you please help me out of this.

thanks.

entry.title: Re: Expand and enhance your knowledge on Sales Bill of Materials (Sales Kits) in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content: @silvia_gaber , Is this 31Q scenario available in 2023 On premise edition? If not, any plan for rolling it out?

entry.title: Re: Buddy App
entry.content: That's an excellent idea. Over to the community managers to execute 😁

entry.title: Re: Creation of a simple Turnover Analysis with Stories in People Analytics
entry.content:
Hi Frank,

Thank you very much for your contribution!


What would you advice, when the Turnover Rate is the very basic calculation: (Termination/Headcount)*100 ?
We want to use this Turnover Rate as a Measure and Termination Event Reasons/Group as a Dimension in a chart/table.

The Headcount Measure is (logically) excluding Terminations and Retirements.
Consequently, as soon as I want to cluster the Turnover Rate according to Termination Reasons, the HC shows zero.

Thank you!

Angela

entry.title: Re: Overview of ILM Policies and Rules
entry.content:
Hi,

We have the scenario that ILM gets stored using DMLT azure packages in to Azure Blob as archive store location. in our ECC ehp8. Any specific steps to be done as part of S4 conversion specific to ILM / DMLT pacakages as pre and post S4 Hana Conversion in to Rise private cloud edition?

Rgds,

Karthik

entry.title: Re: Error triggering an event from ECC to SAP Event Mesh

entry.title: Re: Error triggering an event from ECC to SAP Event Mesh
entry.content: @varunvichare : i am facing the same issue now. I have already done the code page maintenance as well. Were you able to solve this issue and if yes, could you please elaborate how the issue was resolved.Thanks,

entry.title: Re: RE:Job shadow
entry.content:
Dear @Kayln_Sweetman ,

Thank you for your post and nice to meet you virtually.

I would like to point you to this group called Career Corner: https://community.sap.com/t5/career-corner/gh-p/career-corner

Here, you can find articles and discussions about careers, and also some job opportunities. Please review and start a discussion there or apply for a job. Also, this page might give you more insights about jobs at SAP: https://jobs.sap.com/

entry.title: Re: Image based filter for multiple charts with different...
entry.content:
Hi,

setdimensionfilter will overwrite existing filter. I have used 2 different script variables for 2 different images and define the cases (w.r.t variables).Check out the code below for Image 1 and same can be written for Image 2 as well.

 

if(ScriptVariable_1%2===0 && ScriptVariable_2%2!==0)   
{
Chart_1.getDataSource().setDimensionFilter("Product_3e315003an",["[Product_3e315003an].[Product_Catego_3o3x5e06y2].&[PC4]","[Product_3e315003an].[Product_Catego_3o3x5e06y2].&[PC1]"]);
}
else if(ScriptVariable_1%2===0 && ScriptVariable_2%2===0)----First time initialization
{
Chart_1.getDataSource().setDimensionFilter("Product_3e315003an","[Product_3e315003an].[Product_Catego_3o3x5e06y2].&[PC4]");
}
else if(ScriptVariable_2%2!==0)
{Chart_1.getDataSource().removeDimensionFilter("Product_3e315003an");
 Chart_1.getDataSource().setDimensionFilter("Product_3e315003an","[Product_3e315003an].[Product_Catego_3o3x5e06y2].&[PC1]");
}
 else
{
Chart_1.getDataSource().removeDimensionFilter("Product_3e315003an"); 
}
ScriptVariable_1=ScriptVariable_1+1;

entry.title: Re: Mass functionality for Report AI_SDK_SP_AUTO_CLOSE (S...
entry.content:
Hi, 

There is no such feature to mass update incidents using the report AI_SDK_SP_AUTO_CLOSE

You could use this report AI_SDK_SP_AUTO_CLOSE and create a custom implementation to accommodate your requirement. Kindly note that you would be able to change the status of the incident via this report only if you're also able to change it in the Web UI. 

Best Regards, 
Deepa

entry.title: Re: 🇮🇳 SAP CodeJam ABAP: SAP BTP, ABAP Environment & RAP
entry.content:
Finally this worked i recreated new subaccount and instance in different browser.

Thank you so much.

entry.title: Re: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Adding Attachments to a Standard XML
entry.content:
Hi Pedro,

The receiver determines where they want the additional content in the XML file. This receiver can be a tax authority or a business customer. If you are unsure which XML tag needs to be filled, please contact the receiver directly.

Each country decides whether to offer this extensibility option. By checking the help portal documentation for the specific country solution, you can see if this option is available. For example, it is enabled and described for Germany's Customer Invoice: 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/7abc5246a77344b79ec1b1550d94cf92/c3bc8e9af6f444e4a150a40c4c4b9a9f.html?locale=en-US

Best regards,
Karsten Schreyer

entry.title: Re: HTML content is being display as plain text in HTML C...
entry.content:
Hi,
Starting from Focused Insights support package ST-OST 12 Rich Text Editor has been introduced in OCC dashboard configuration settings. In earlier support packages i.e. below SP 12 the HTML text has to be created in the backend using report program to display HTML text.
From SP12 onwards HTML content can be directly created in the Rich Text editor itself. In the Editor we need not write HTML Tags. It is the standard feature of Rich Text Editor.The formating has to be done using toolbar or you can directly paste the formatted HTML content.Hence, Please do not insert HTML tags in the editor.

Please check the link on the enhancement done on HTML Renderer

entry.title: 重要提醒：SAP Analytics Cloud 经典故事和分析应用的相关变动
entry.content:
简介

故事和分析应用的经典体验是我们产品的原始用户界面，多年来一直为我们的客户和合作伙伴提供分析服务。然而，随着技术的发展和用户期望的变化，我们决定逐步弃用经典体验和其他非战略功能，并用优化体验来替代。 优化体验是一个集故事、分析应用程序和数字化董事会议厅核心功能于一体的平台，它沿用了现有的许可证结构。优化体验通过提供一体化的用户体验，为客户带来了更多产品创新、更高的灵活性以及更好的性能和易用性。

这篇博客将为您介绍弃用经典体验的原因、优化体验的优势以及转换的关键时间节点。我们希望这篇文章能帮助您在充分利用优化体验的同时为变更做好准备。

为什么要弃用经典体验？

经典体验一直是我们产品可靠、稳定的用户界面，但它也存在一些影响用户体验和性能的限制及缺点。以下是弃用的一些主要原因：

  • 性能和可用性挑战：我们收到的反馈表明，我们的客户在使用经典体验时，在性能和可用性上遇到了一些挑战，这些挑战将影响他们对产品的应用程度。
  • 一体化要求：由于故事、分析应用程序和数字化董事会议厅在经典体验中相对独立，用户经常需要对应该使用哪种资产而感到困惑。同时这种独立性也意味着一些不必要的开发工作量，并且最终用户也会因为不同资产的使用而有着截然不同的体验。
  • 创新要求：我们一直致力于交付有关优化体验的创新。这意味着，继续使用经典体验的客户将无法从我们基于客户反馈（客户圆桌会议或 Influence Portal ）所做的新功能和产品改进中受益。

优化体验的优势是什么？

“优化体验” 是一个集故事、分析应用程序和数字化董事会议厅核心功能于一体的平台，它沿用了现有的许可证结构。它通过提供一体化的用户体验，为客户带来了更多产品创新、更高的灵活性以及更好的性能和易用性。

更多关于优化体验优势的详细信息，请阅读资源区内的相关博客文章。

时间安排和影响

我们已经开始逐步弃用经典体验和其他不在我们战略范围内的功能。这些功能包括：

  • 经典故事（包括网格页面和智能发现）
  • 分析应用（与故事集成）（博客）
  • 经典数字化董事会议厅（与故事集成）（博客）
  • 经典故事和分析应用中的低采用功能（文档）
  • 搜索到洞察（在 Cloud Foundry 租户中由“Just Ask”替代）
  • 分配与分摊（由“数据操作分配与分摊”步骤替代）
  • 兴趣点

我们将通过“多步走”的方式来帮助客户完成整个过程。实际上，产品中已经提供了一些功能，例如：

  • 在分配模块中，新增了一个弹窗，该弹窗指导用户使用新的数据操作分配步骤。

 

  • 随着 2024 年第二季度 SAP Analytics Cloud QRC 版本的发布（该版本已于 2024 年 5 月 18 日星期六向所有客户提供），我们在产品内为经典故事和分析应用程序引入了停用警告。

 

为了进一步了解变更的影响，以下是一些需要注意的时间节点（免责声明：这是目前的计划状态，SAP 可能会随时更改）：

  • 自 2024 年第四季度 QRC 更新起：新创建的租户将无法访问上述非战略功能。这意味着已创建的内容包将无法导入不受支持的资产。
  • 截至 2025 年上半年：现有客户将无法创建或复制不支持的资产。对于这些资产，客户仍可以查看、编辑和共享。系统管理员将有一个新的配置选项，可以设置在一定时间内对未查看的资产进行自动删除。
  • 截至 2025 年下半年：客户将无法再访问不受支持的资产。您需要在截止日期之前将内容进行迁移。

如何准备？

我们理解这一转换可能需要您进行一些计划和准备。在转换经典故事和分析应用时，您可以通过以下步骤来准备转换：

  • 阅读“资源”章节中的博客文章，了解有关优化故事体验及其优势的更多信息。
  • 了解有关优化体验中不可用的功能的更多信息。可通过此处的应用内文档获取。
  • 使用工具栏中“文件-保存”下的“转换为优化设计体验”选项，开始将现有的经典故事和分析应用程序迁移到优化故事体验。

总结

将经典体验停用并转向优化体验是我们做出的一项战略决策。这样的转变将为我们的客户和合作伙伴带来长期的益处。我们感谢您在这个过程中的理解与合作，我们致力于为您提供最出色的用户体验和性能。我们鼓励并推荐您开始尝试转移一些经典故事和分析应用设计，如果您在此过程当中遇到了一些挑战，您可以在留言区留言，我和我的团队将为您提供必要的支持。

资源

优化体验中的亮点新功能

  • 组合
  • 替换模型
  • 演示模式

分析应用的集成优势

  • 体验新的优化设计体验
  • 统一故事和分析应用

性能和可用性改进

  • 活动视口渲染
  • 精简查看器
  • 主要改进和功能
  • 性能和可用性改进

原文链接：https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/important-notice-changes-in-sap-analytics-cloud-classic-stories-amp/ba-p/13705220

entry.title: Re: New Handling Unit Transactions in S/4HANA - COWBPACK,...
entry.content:
Dear Chris,

I guess you mean the transactions HU02, COWBHUWE, COPAWA and COP2? These transactions exist in S/4HANA as well as S/4HANA Public Cloud. In Public Cloud these transactions are started via Fiori Launchpad as so-called Web GUIs but they are the same transactions you know from ECC.

Best regards,
Barış

entry.title: Re: My Code Connect 2024 Experience
entry.content:
Great write-up!

It was awesome to meet you and everybody else 👋🏻👋🏻

entry.title: Re: Show Tcode on Fiori Tile
entry.content: Hmmm... I'm afraid that's incorrect. As explained in the blog you can add or override information on the tile texts using the Launchpad App Manager. Also there is a SAP Note to mass update tiles with the transaction code - see 3289200 - Fill Tcode in Info-Field for Synchronization with local replication / embedded scenario

entry.title: Re: How to deal with unit of measures when calling OData services from SAP S/4 HANA in SAP Business
entry.content:
Thanks @Andre_Fischer for the info/the blog.

I was able to solve the problem ~3 years ago by changing the type to MSEHI. 

Unfortunately, the workaround does not work for all cases.
If the API is used to read a unit of measure that exists on the source system but not in the BTP ABAP environment, the conversion continues to dump.

Unfortunately, it is not possible to maintain the units of measure (standard, non YY_*/ZZ_*) in the BTP ABAP environment.
We were given the option of an “expert import” (import by SAP Support).
Unfortunately, the issue has been dragging on for about a year now (with an effort of a few minutes) and does not appear to be practicable for a later customer-productive approach.

Do you have any ideas on how the last gap can be closed?
Will it be possible to maintain the data yourself?
What would be the right approach to initiate this if it is not yet in planning? (Influence request?)


Kind regards,
Alexander

entry.title: Re: Scale-down HANA Cloud instance
entry.content:
Please take a look at this.
3253081 - How to: change the size of HANA Cloud instance?
https://me.sap.com/notes/0003253081

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
entry.content:
Are you ready to take your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition implementation project to the next level? Join our online e-learning session offered by the SAP Enterprise Support Academy to gain a comprehensive overview of SAP Cloud ALM for implementation and learn how it can help you manage your implementation project from start to finish.

 Here are some of the benefits of joining our e-learning session:

  • Gain insight into SAP Cloud ALM for implementation
  • Understand the core capabilities of SAP Cloud ALM for implementation
  • Learn how to effectively use SAP Cloud ALM for implementation in the context of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • Get a demonstration of the tool and see how it guides all necessary implementation activities, such as project team onboarding, fit-to-standard workshops, testing, and project documentation
  • Learn how to request your SAP Cloud ALM tenant and get started with implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

By participating in this e-learning session, you will be equipped with the knowledge and tools necessary to streamline your implementation project and ensure its success.

 Don't miss out on this opportunity to sharpen your skills and take advantage of the powerful capabilities of SAP Cloud ALM for implementation. Register now for our upcoming e-learning session and take your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition implementation to new heights!

Note: Before you can access the link below, a one-time registration to the SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support, is required. A detailed step-by-step guide on how to register can be found here.


Register here:

SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

entry.title: Re: Problem with ALV column displaying as exponent instead of quantity (len15, dec3)
entry.content: case when vbap.kwmeng = 0 then 0 else vbap.kwmeng end as OpenQty

entry.title: Re: gl change log table
entry.content: Your answer just solved my problem, thanks

entry.title: New Release of SAP Data and Analytics Advisory Methodology
entry.content:
The SAP Data and Analytics Advisory Methodology was introduced over a year ago (see blog). Today, I am happy to announce the new release of the methodology providing several enhancements and new content.

In this blog, I will provide an overview of the changes we are delivering with the new release.

The underlying approach has not changed and has been successfully applied in several customer engagements. We had some findings we reflected in the approach, especially in the following areas:

Phase III was adapted to streamline the architecture development process.

  • Introduction of “Solution Concept”
  • Modernized Data & Analytics Capability Model

Existing gaps in phase IV we closed

  • Introduction of “Data & Analytics Maturity Assessment”
  • Update on SAP BTP Reference Architectures for Data & Analytics

Finally, we will use SAP Help to provide a detailed description of the procedures and tools used in the four phases of the methodology.

Updates in Phase III

We have adapted the architecture development process by introducing a “solution concept” from the SAP Enterprise Architecture Framework (SAP EAF) methodology. A solution concept diagram provides a high-level representation of the solution that is envisioned to meet the requirements of the architecture engagement. This helps to structure the target architecture in a generic way by using architecture building blocks. This conceptional architecture is the baseline for the following steps where each architecture building block is replaced by IT solutions to build the final solution architecture.

The following diagram provides an example of a solution concept.

Data & Analytics Maturity Assessment (Phase IV)

The methodology has a clear focus on developing a target architecture for data-driven business outcomes. Nevertheless, data governance and organizational aspects are equally important to be successful in becoming a data-driven company. Therefore, they need to be investigated at least at a high-level and the necessary actions incorporated into the roadmap created in the final step.

Therefore, we introduce a high-level data governance maturity assessment based on a far more comprehensive SAP Data Management Framework provided by SAP Lab. The aim is to identify data governance areas where maturity is insufficient and define appropriate actions that feed into the roadmap.

We have selected 15 focus topics from five dimensions (i.e., data strategy, data & KPI’s, data architecture, processes and organization) of the SAP Data Management Framework that are most relevant from a governance and organizational perspective. In our methodology, we propose to select and assess only those focus topics which are most relevant to the architecture context that was discussed in the previous phases.

Each selected focus topic is then discussed in terms of current and required maturity level. Five maturity levels exist ranging from “initial” to “data-driven”. Each level is described in the context of the focus topic.

Here is one example from the dimension “data strategy” covering the focus topics “data strategy management” and “data literacy”:

We will describe phase IV of the methodology in a subsequent blog, where we will provide more details on how to apply the maturity assessment and, as a final step, develop the roadmap to realize the target architecture and related actions.

It is important to mention at this stage that this maturity assessment is rather a generic investigation that is intended to provide a first perspective on data governance and organizational aspects. If the result of the assessment indicates that a deeper investigation is required, we refer to specialized frameworks from SAP or our partners.

New Data & Analytics Capability Model

We have reworked the Data & Analytics Capability Model to reflect state-of-the-art capabilities from the data & analytics practice. We also skipped a capability level to reduce complexity and make it more applicable in the methodology.

The capability model is organized around six data and analytics-relevant technology areas (blue areas) that contain the respective capabilities:

  • Analytics and Planning: capabilities that use data analysis and forecasting techniques to inform strategic decision-making and optimize business performance
  • Data Management: capabilities that allow the processing, storage, organization, and maintenance of all types of data to ensure its accuracy, integrity, security, and accessibility for analysis and decision-making purposes.
  • Metadata Management: capabilities that enable organizing, documenting, and controlling the metadata of an organization's data assets to ensure accurate and consistent information across systems and enable effective data governance and analytics.
  • Data Quality Management: capabilities that ensure that data is accurate, complete, consistent, and reliable throughout its lifecycle to support effective decision-making and business operations.
  • Data Integration: capabilities that enable accessing, combining, and harmonizing data from various sources into a unified and consistent format for further processing and analysis.
  • Security & Compliance: capabilities reflecting measures and protocols to protect sensitive information from unauthorized access, breaches, and ensure adherence to relevant laws and regulations.

Data & Analytics capabilities in these areas can be mapped to IT solutions from SAP or other vendors and are the baseline for developing the target architecture as described in the previous blog.

We indicate other technology domains like AI, application development or process automation that can provide supplementary capabilities if required (grey areas).

The technical capabilities are complemented by organizational capabilities around data governance and architecture (orange area). The idea is to drive the discussion toward the capabilities required to successfully enable the value creation for which the target architecture is intended. This can be considered as part of the analysis of data governance and organizational aspects introduced in the previous section.

Update on Data & Analytics use case patterns and SAP BTP Reference Architectures

The methodology provides a “fast lane” in phase III to accelerate the process of developing a target architecture using SAP BTP Reference Architectures. This option exists if the customer use cases analyzed in phase II match the data & analytics use case patterns that are realized by SAP-centric reference architectures (see blog phase III).

We have reorganized the structure of those use case patterns that focus on data provisioning and call them “Data Foundation Patterns” (see bottom area of illustration below). We included the “Business Data Fabric” as an overarching pattern, a concept that was introduced with SAP Datasphere. It enables organizations to seamlessly access and interact with a semantically rich data layer regardless of data location or format. As it combines data management, integration and governance capabilities, the related SAP BTP Reference Architecture provides the complete picture.

The underlying use case patterns and reference architectures for Data Management, Data Integration and Data Governance) will be more detailed as they cover specific aspects of business data fabric. 

I would like to point out that SAP now provides such Reference Architectures not only for Data & Analytics but for all relevant technology domains covered by SAP BTP, e.g.
• Artificial Intelligence
• Automation
• Integration
• App Development

SAP Reference Architectures are published in the SAP Discovery Centre. Here you will also find SAP BTP Reference Architectures that integrating Hyperscaler und partner solutions, confirming SAP’s goal to integrate into an Open Data Ecosystem.

Documentation of the methodology

The updated PDF presentation and PPT template of the methodology are available for download in our SAP Work Zone. For those who do not have access yet, please send an email to sap-data-analytics-methodology@sap.com. The methodology and related material can be used free of charge.

As with the other two SAP BTP methodologies, ISA-M and AEM, detailed documentation for the SAP Data & Analytics Advisory Methodology will be available in the SAP Help Portal soon. Here you will find a detailed description of the four phases with more examples and best practices.

Acknowledgements

I would like to take the opportunity to express my gratitude to all colleagues from SAP and our partners Camelot ITLab, Infomotion, PWC and IBsolution. Thank you very much for your support and for providing your expertise to us in the last months.

Special thanks go to:

  • form SAP: Mohamed Judi, Naveen Gudur, Phillip Kleemann, Alina Novosolova, Fabian Lehmann, Marco van Horck, Robert Stam, Brett Philpott, Matthias Stemmler, Karsten Haldenwang, and so many more
  • from Camelot: Ahmad Mashood, Thorsten Warnecke, Andreas Staudenecker
  • from Infomotion: Peter Baumann

Finally, I would like to thank PostFinance, the first customer that fully applied the methodology. They allowed us using some of their results as references.

Next up will be a blog on phase IV of the methodology where I will look into data governance & roadmap development in more detail.

entry.title: Re: Modify Z* Db table not working
entry.content: No. Create a test table and try, you will see that it works fine. Then check what can be different. Note that maybe you are confused because you didn't commit, or you are looking at the data in another client. When it's so basic, you should try different things till you find the solution. Only you can find.

entry.title: Re: Invalid column name error while loading DTP
entry.content:
Hi 

Could you look into the below SAP Notes and see if your issue is same?

3414081 - CompositeProvider activation fails with "invalid column name: " error

entry.title: Re: Replicating Opt-Outs from external webforms to Market...
entry.content:
Hi Isabelle,

I disagree with the statement that 3 contacts with the same info@address could not be quality data.

In a B2B context this often happens. For example in retail. You can very well have 3 accounts for 3 stores on 3 different addresses, but which are owned by the same shop owner. The email address on Account level could then very well be 3 times info@ownercompany.com. On the contact level, in theory the "same" owner contact should be linked to the three accounts, and if "relationships" have been activated in the marketing system then also on marketing side there could be one single contact with a relationship to the three accounts. But in reality it also happens that sales creates the contact multiple times on the c4c side and you end up with 3 times owner@ownercompany.com on the contact level too.

Also, there are cases where sending marketing communication to an info@address is a valid use case. In B2B, you can send mails to such addresses without a marketing permission, so this is one of the few ways to be able to reach potential prospects before getting contact-level-info including a permission from them.

entry.title: Re: Objects are imported but not generated when moved to production system
entry.content: Please show the images, don't attach them, that's especially important if there are many, to avoid click all to show them.

entry.title: S/4 HANA Public Cloud: Event-Based Costing - Costing Sheet and Activity Types
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community, 

 

Is it possible on SAP S/4 HANA Cloud to exclude the use of Activity Type for Labor Costs in production costing?

This is with the idea of using Costing Sheets fully for all production costs both in actual and standard costing for Labor and Overhead costs. 

How can we apply? 

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: My Code Connect 2024 Experience
entry.content: It was nice finally meeting you and all the other guys from down under! See you hopefully next time!

entry.title: Re: Is there any Online machine learning algorithm for complex dynamical system
entry.content:
First of all, it is not clear, that this minimum exists. If e.g. x_i = y_i and the x_i are defined on whole R, there is no minimum.

If you have a large set of training data you can train a neural network to approximate the function. Applying a hill climber alg. you could find an approximation of a local min. of this function  if one exists.

entry.title: SAP DM - Difference between Quantity Confirmaion and Yield Confirmation
entry.content:
Dear all experts,

I'd like to know the difference between "Production Order Quantity Confirmation" and "Production Order Yield Confirmation".

  • What triggers these confirmation?(Is it when each operation is completed or SFC is completed?)
  • When these confirmation are integrated to S/4, is GR triggered?

Regards,

Koichi Okamoto

entry.title: Process order number range skipping from 40010 to 40020 and after few orders again its taking 40013
entry.content:
Hi

We have a buffer on process order number ranges as it is recommended by default. But while creating orders , for e.g it created 40010 then it would take 40020 but after few orders it again goes back and takes 40013. We want to avoid this but also do not want to impact the performance of the system considering the number of users.

please recommend a solution!

entry.title: Re: New Handling Unit Transactions in S/4HANA - COWBPACK,...
entry.content: Hi Baris, thanks for your answer. There is the discussion about general HU Management. If we move from ECC to Hana (with SAP WM or SAP EWM), if there are changes in the HU Transactions? The only change which I saw is the COWBPACK Transaction which is replaced by COP1 or COP2. All other transactions will be the same is that correct? The HU_CREATE is a function module. There is the discussion at the moment, if this module is also available in the S/4HANA world? Thanks a lot. BR, Chris

entry.title: Token Exchange - RFC8693
entry.content:
Hello Team,

We need our desktop-based products to manage long-running tasks with user sessions i.e. file uploading by allowing the security token exchange for a longer period. Once the user is logged in to the system and has a valid token to kick off the tasks, the system keeps allowing exchanging tokens until the task is completed. Auth 2.0 defines the Token Exchange strategy to handle these use cases. It covers the delegation and impersonation semantics.

I am looking to if CDC has or supports the token exchange model that Thermo Fisher Identity platform can abstract or integrate to support our desktop-based model for long-running tasks.

Reference: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc8693

Thanks,

Dinesh

entry.title: Multicursor in Eclipse (ADT) by search dialog
entry.content:
Hi,
I'm trying to jump back and forth between Visual Studio Code and Eclipse a little less. And I mainly miss the multicursor function and especially the setting of multiple cursors via search. Eclipse basically offers the function in the search dialog, but it is always inactive for me. Regardless of whether I am in CDS or other source code. Is there a trick or is it not yet implemented by ADT?

entry.title: SAP Utilities Roadmap and Customer Collaboration Espresso Session - Watch the Recording
entry.content:
Dear Utilities Community,

We have expanded our SAP for Energy & Utilities Espresso Session, which are half-hour information sessions, to include further topics. Recently, we provided information about our Utilities Roadmap.

In this session, Holger Schweinfurth and Jelisaveta Nikolic provided an overview of the utilities roadmap and customer collaboration. They covered topics such as influencing the SAP standard roadmap, utilities roadmap, customer engagement, beta testing, early adopter care, and continuous influence. Get insights on  planned investment areas in utilities, including asset management, customer experience, regulatory requirements, and energy transition and sustainability.

The next Espresso session is scheduled for June 27, 4pm CET and covers the SAP Oil, Gas, and Energy Roadmap and Customer Collaboration.

 

 

 

 

 

Thank you to @holger_schweinfurth and @JelisavetaN for the informative session!

entry.title: Re: 🇮🇳 SAP CodeJam ABAP: SAP BTP, ABAP Environment & RAP
entry.content:
Hello Rahul,

Please try creating a new subaccount or rather start from scratch. I got stuck while lunching and navigating from home page to cart and started again from a new subaccount and it works for me. 

Thanks

P2003184310

entry.title: Re: Third party delivery process for subcontracting raw m...
entry.content: Thank you for your confirmation. Duly noted. But I woulid like to know the operation how to delivery subcontracting raw material directly to subcontracting vendor. The note you mentioned is about the operation how to delivery subcontracting finished material directly to customer. Could you please tell us the above operation? Same as the third party finished goods, the operation is not supported in S4HC?

entry.title: Do you want to know how you can unlock the potential of AI?
entry.content:
🌀In today's rapidly evolving digital landscape, everyone is keen on integrating Artificial Intelligence into their workflows; yet starting with a clear purpose is crucial. But: How to know where and how to start? ✨

The SAP AppHaus created two innovative workshop formats, the Business AI Explore and Business AI Discover/Design, crafted to guide people in identifying and defining impactful AI use cases tailored to their specific needs. The workshops emphasize that first of all, you need to understand the existing business challenges. Only then you can find out how AI might enhance your workflows and operations.

In this webinar, Karen Detken, creator of both the Business AI Discover/Design and the Business AI Explore workshop, will offer a comprehensive overview of both formats, provide practical examples, actionable tips, and share where to access the materials for conducting your own workshops.

📣Join us on June 18th to discover how you can effectively harness generative AI, and what you need consider beforehand.

Add to calendar: https://url.sap/8xtf5s

YouTube link: https://youtube.com/live/8ks3BXqH5XE?feature=share

entry.title: Re: 1H2024 - Hide the Required Learning Section on the Ne...
entry.content:
Hello,

As mentioned in the Hide the Required Learning Section on the New Learning Home Page | SAP Help Portal,

You can hide the 'Required Learning' section for specific user groups and organizations using the Hide the Required Learning section if no training is assigned option from the following pages respectively:

  • Learning Administration > People > User Groups > Advanced Settings

  • Learning Administration > People > Organizations > Advanced Settings

You would have to set the element “hideRequiredLearningSectionOnLearningPortal” to “true” in the “LMS_ADMIN” configuration before enabling the setting in Advanced settings.

However, Currently you cannot enable/disable this setting via Organization connector as it is not available yet

entry.title: Usage Decision QA11 (321 movement type)
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

user done usage decision for 280pcs with 321 movmt type from quality to unrestricted, but  the stock is present in Q (quality control stock) in WM. i want the stock in Available stock.
can anyone please guide me how to do it?

thank you in advance

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Code: Java support
entry.content: Would also like to know if SAP Build Code is supporting Java and how.

entry.title: Re: SQL Database to datasphere vis DP connetor
entry.content:
Hello @AkshaySakhpara ,

All steps for connecting to an SQL Server are available in the Microsoft SQL Server Connections | SAP Help Portal

If you want to use Data Flows, the DPAgent is not required.

Where is your SQL Server, is in the cloud or on-prem? 

If it is cloud, have you opened the access to the DSP server in your cloud provider?

If it is on-prem, if the server/port is not published to the internet, have you installed SAP Cloud Connector in your on-prem network to allow access from DSP?

Regards,

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA public cloud : Payment advice not sent to...
entry.content:
Hi,

Could you please check the settings in the following places?

Check the status of your output items in the Manage Automatic Payments app. If an output item has been sent out successfully, it should be in status "Completed". If your output item is still in status "In Preparation", you should display the message log to see if there is any issue with your item.

Make sure an email address has been maintained in at least one of the following places:

  • Business partner master data
  • The Email Recipient determination step in the Output Parameter Determination app

as described in Configuring the Output of Payment Advice Forms | SAP Help Portal. 

Good luck.

entry.title: Re: Is there any way to embed a program or transaction into another program?
entry.content: Thank you for your answer, but this is not the result I want. What I want may not be possible in SAP.

entry.title: Re: Is there any way to embed a program or transaction into another program?
entry.content: Thanks, I think you're right, it may not be possible.

entry.title: How to set the Alternative key in RAP similar to the BOPF Alternative key concept?
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

We have Alternative keys in BOPF, these alternative keys, will help in uniqueness check automatically for non-key fields when the key is UUID.

How can we achieve the same alternative keys in RAP development?

Thank you, in Advance.

entry.title: Re: SBO Mail not working in New installation 10.0 FP2402
entry.content:
I think the problem comes because it is a new installation and the poor configuration made in the SLD.

I attach an image, SLD.PNG in which you can see that you do not keep the credentials to the database or the repository path, the repository user name I have added it.

I think I will have to add a new server here in the SLD with this correctly covered information and verify if it leaves it recorded.
It would be good to know that XML file and route keep this information to edit or tune it.

entry.title: SAP B1 SDK Load system form from xml - form already exists
entry.content:
Dear experts,

I have saved a system form with extra controls as XML. I am then trying to load the XML and get the following error: Form - already exists [66000-11]. Form Unique Id : 'F_31

This is the code I am using:

private void UpdateFormByXML(string sFileName, string sFormUID)
        {
            try
            {
                string sXPath = "Application//forms//action//form//@uid";
                //string sPath = System.Environment.CurrentDirectory + "\\";
                string sPath = System.IO.Directory.GetParent(System.Windows.Forms.Application.StartupPath).ToString();
                sPath = System.IO.Directory.GetParent(sPath).ToString();

                System.Xml.XmlDocument xDoc = new System.Xml.XmlDocument();

                //xDoc.Load(sPath + sFileName);
                xDoc.Load(sPath + "\\" + sFileName);

                System.Xml.XmlNode xNode = xDoc.SelectSingleNode(sXPath);
                xNode.InnerText = sFormUID;

                string sXML = xDoc.InnerXml.ToString();

                globals.SBO_Application.LoadBatchActions(ref sXML);
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                globals.SBO_Application.SetStatusBarMessage(ex.Message, SAPbouiCOM.BoMessageTime.bmt_Short, true);
            }
        }

 

 

                       if (pVal.FormType == 60150 & pVal.EventType != SAPbouiCOM.BoEventTypes.et_FORM_UNLOAD && pVal.Before_Action == true)
                        {
                            oCECForm = globals.SBO_Application.Forms.GetFormByTypeAndCount(pVal.FormType, pVal.FormTypeCount);

                            if (pVal.EventType == SAPbouiCOM.BoEventTypes.et_FORM_LOAD && pVal.Before_Action == true)
                            {
                                UpdateFormByXML("CEC.xml", pVal.FormUID);
                            }
                        }

 

Could someone please help?

 

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: SBO Mail not working in New installation 10.0 FP2402
entry.content: The port is used by default in the installation https://server-name:40000/ControlCenter/

entry.title: Could not commit transaction: Error -1 detected during transaction '' (AWOR)
entry.content:
Hello,

We developed an addon that automate the production order posting process by fetch the [Released] POs and show them as a form, and has a button to select all/dissellect in order to release and receive and complete production process in batch.

But the case is that; after installing this addon, we became unable to perform any action on the Production Order doctype, whenever try to post, duplicate PO or change the status for existing ones, we're getting this error:

Could not commit transaction: Error -1 detected during transaction '' (AWOR)

In order to troubleshoot this issue, we tried the following but got no solution:

  1. Delete the addon file from Extension Manager, SAP B1 Client, related addon file in Windows Explorer
  2. The Transaction Notification SP has no code or modifications
  3. The PO screen has no FMS
  4. As the addon was installed on a sub Database Server from the main database server, we deleted that Database Server from Control Center in SLD, deleted all related databases include SBO-COMMON, deleted the SQL instance itself and all other related files and temp files in the machine
  5. Reinstall brandnew SQL instance, SLD Database Server
  6. Compared the stored procedures in the SLD databases between 2 different versions one before installing the addon and another after installing it but there was no any difference
  7. Compared the storoed procedures in the SBO-COMMON database between 2 different versions one before installing the addon and another after installing it but there was no any difference

What would be solution for this case?

Regards

entry.title: Re: Questions re: "Multi-model with SAP HANA Cloud" developer challenge
entry.content:
Hi Vitaliy,

I am getting the below error with Step-9. Can you please help me here ?

entry.title: New E-learning: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Procure-To-Pay Overview
entry.content:
Are you a SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition user looking to enhance your knowledge of the procure-to-pay processes? Or maybe you are new to the system and eager to learn how to streamline your procurement operations? No matter your level of experience, our online e-learning course is the perfect opportunity for you to deep dive into the functionality of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Procure-To-Pay processes.

During this e-learning course, offered by the SAP Enterprise Support Academy, you will gain a comprehensive understanding of the end-to-end operational procurement process, from the initial purchase requisition to the payment of the supplier invoice. Our expert instructors will guide you through each step, providing detailed explanations and a system demonstration to ensure you fully comprehend the processes and best practices.

By the end of the course, you will not only have a strong grasp of the procure-to-pay processes but will also have a clear understanding of the future roadmap, innovations, and additional resources available to further optimize your procurement operations with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

This e-learning course is designed to accommodate your schedule, allowing you to access the content at your own pace and convenience. Whether you prefer to complete the course in a few sittings or take it step-by-step over several days, the flexibility of online learning allows you to tailor your experience to suit your needs.

Join us for this informative and engaging e-learning opportunity and take your knowledge of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Procure-To-Pay processes to the next level. Whether you're a procurement professional, IT specialist, or business user, this course is guaranteed to provide you with the insights and skills needed to optimize your use of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Enroll now and embark on an exciting journey towards mastering the procure-to-pay processes with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.


Note: Before you can access the link below, a one-time registration to the SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support, is required. A detailed step-by-step guide on how to register can be found here.

 Register here:

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Procure-To-Pay Overview

entry.title: Re: Tips to add your SAP Build Apps application to different BTP environments - Part 4

entry.content: The pleasure meeting you was about thaaaaat big!

entry.title: Valuation area MI## unable to be locked
entry.content: Hi, I received the message "Valuation area MI## unable to be locked" when I ran CKMSTART. I have done OMX2, OMX3, and OMX1. Everything seems ok. Please help.

entry.title: Re: Query using $[USER] changes to the id in the query on SAP b1 on HANA
entry.content: Any idea how to solve it? I have similar issue in ver 10.0. While saving query It shows in Where clause $[USER] but later on when you edit query it returns your UserId

entry.title: Re: definition of Full User Equivalents FUE user rights
entry.content: thanks for this information, its very useful. one question is, if a user is active and has roles in DEV and QA only in S4Hana Cloud (locked in PROD tenant) are they still considered as a FUE?

entry.title: Client copy
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Please let us know the steps to copy master and transactional data from one client to another client in S4 hana public cloud. will implementing our S4hana tenant we had opted for demo data, we only get one client is it, when we opt for demo data?? 

 Regards,

Bharath.G

Regards.

entry.title: Re: Loading data to Planning enabled Model through Analyt...
entry.content:
Hi  William_Yu

Thank you for immediate response.

this blog works file direct mapping to excel columns to Model Dimension and defaults FactData/PrivatefactFData API ? https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/how-to-upload-data-to-a-public-or-private-plann...

But, we have some requirement unformatted business excel data, Business manual data entry excel,  using blog help to upload excel to table data  https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/introducing-fileuploadxls-the-ultimate-excel-custom-widget-for-sap/ba-p/13704913

This way unformatted excel method getDataAsJson()  excel as table data and re-arrange the excel data customize again model data format as formatted  column value to getPlanning().setUserInput & getPlanning().submitData() script to post data.

data posting sample blog :https://insightcubes.com/blog/sap-analytics-designer/overview-of-the-getplanningapi-and-related-functions-with-sap-analytics-designer/

script looks expected setUserInput formatted:

all looks right but getting error like :

Thank you,

Samy

entry.content: how to resolved the problem?

entry.title: Re: Valuation area MI## unable to be locked
entry.content:
Hi @pook_sowyee

3248145 - CKMSTART is not working for plants due to error C+023 - SAP for Me

Thanks & regards

Sanil Bhandari

entry.title: Re: Dynamic parallel processing in SAP S/4HANA flexible w...
entry.content: There are 3 callback methods in the runtime controll class for steps: IF_SWF_FLEX_IFS_RUN_APPL_STEP~BEFORE_CREATION_CALLBACK IF_SWF_FLEX_IFS_RUN_APPL_STEP~ON_CREATION_CALLBACK IF_SWF_FLEX_IFS_RUN_APPL_STEP~AFTER_COMPLETION_CALLBACK

entry.title: Re: EBS underpayment processing with deductions for bank...
entry.content: Hi, I'm facing with the same issue. The amount in 2 area posting is taken from /OCMT/ description instead the real amount on bank statement. Someone resolve this issue?

entry.title: Re: Can a custom event trigger a process in SAP Build Process Automation?
entry.content:
Hi Everyone, 

Yes this feature is planned for Q3 but it has to be a Cloud Event that can be configured in Event Mesh. 

Thanks & Regards,

Win

entry.title: Re: New E-learning: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Procure-To-Pay Overview
entry.content:
Hi @silvia_gaber,

How I can find this e-leanings for other modules like asset management? Is there any central place in SAP blogs for this?

BR,

Nima

entry.title: Re: Wrong GL is debited in Landing Cost JE
entry.content:
Hi,

Price difference GL gets hot only in cases where entire stock is not available.

Value gets posted to this GL basis availability.

Please check the date and time if user have transferred or consumed a portion of stock.

Check GL determination too for safer side.

Try simulating scenario in test DB

entry.title: Re: New Handling Unit Transactions in S/4HANA - COWBPACK,...
entry.content: Hi Chris, general information about Handling Unit Management in S/4HANA Public Cloud: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/32da8359c8ee4e8b8e8c5e15cacba5aa/b4c3ead97179498b900e7a3e808c03af.html?q=handling%20unit%20management Function Modules like HU_CREATE are not available in the Public Cloud. But instead you can use APIs: - Local API (Steampunk): https://api.sap.com/bointerface/I_HANDLINGUNITTP - Remote API (OData): https://api.sap.com/api/HANDLINGUNIT_0001/overview Best regards, Barış

entry.title: Re: Submissions for "SAP HANA Cloud multi-model" developer challenge: Week 1 - Setup
entry.content: Yesterday, the keystore just wouldn't respond and threw errors, today, no issues:

entry.content:

I have a query here regarding standard Content for S/4HANA Cloud.

entry.title: Re: New E-learning: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Procure-To-Pay Overview
entry.content:
Hi Nima,

in SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support we also have sessions about assets management. Here a link to the course.

Please make sure you are authorized and registered first and then you can access, search and join the sessions.

Full details on the SAP Enterprise Support Academy pages: SAP Enterprise Support Academy
Customer Sign-Up: SAP Learning Hub

Best regards, Silvia
